summary: Review of shipping.py reveals a simple shipping calculation function with a free shipping threshold.

claims.text: File shipping.py contains a calculate_shipping function with FREE_SHIPPING_THRESHOLD_CENTS constant
Function returns 0 cents shipping when subtotal meets threshold, otherwise returns 700 cents
No input validation or error handling present in the function
claims.inference_basis: Function signature shows int parameter with no validation logic
limitations: Cannot verify edge cases without test evidence

claims.text: Hardcoded shipping rate of 700 cents exists

findings.title: Missing Input Validation
findings.description: The calculate_shipping function accepts subtotal_cents as int but does not validate input range or type
findings.impact: Could cause unexpected behavior if invalid values are passed
findings.recommendation: Add input validation to ensure subtotal_cents is non-negative integer
limitations: Cannot verify current validation behavior without test evidence

findings.title: Hardcoded Shipping Rate
findings.description: Shipping cost of 700 cents is hardcoded rather than configurable
findings.impact: Changes require code modification rather than configuration
findings.recommendation: Consider making shipping rate configurable via constant or parameter

next_safe_action: Add input validation for subtotal_cents parameter and consider making shipping rate configurable
phase_limitations: Cannot verify test coverage or runtime behavior without additional evidence
